message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding trans_metadata support: editing InternationalizationExtension.parse() (metadata param parsing in the while-loop over trans tag params) and _make_node() (new metadata dict param + set output_node.trans_metadata attr)

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding domain= parameter support to trans tag. Will edit: InternationalizationExtension.parse() (parse domain="string" param in trans tag header), InternationalizationExtension._make_node() (generate dgettext/dngettext calls when domain given), InternationalizationExtension._install_callables() (install dgettext/dngettext globals).

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() param loop
code: # Proposed handling for metadata params (note/created/modified), inserted
# right after the 'token.value in variables' duplicate-check and before the
# existing 'if parser.stream.current.type == \"assign\":' branch:

        if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
            if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )
            next(parser.stream)
            value_token = parser.stream.current
            value = parser.parse_expression()
            if not isinstance(value, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(value.value, str):
                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.",
                    value_token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )
            metadata[token.value] = value.value
            continue

# Also need `metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}` initialized alongside `trimmed = None`,
# and the comma-check at top of loop widened from `if variables:` to
# `if variables or metadata:` (plus your domain var if it's also excluded from
# `variables`) so commas are correctly required/optional across mixed param kinds
# (see test_trimmed_vars: `{%- trans trimmed x="world" %}` relies on omitted comma
# when only trimmed/notrimmed has been seen so far).
note: This is my proposed insertion point/logic for note/created/modified. Since domain= likely has the same shape (name token + assign + string-literal check), can you share your exact domain-parsing snippet + how you plan to widen the comma-check condition, so we write one identical merged parse() body? Also need your _make_node() signature change to append mine after.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() param loop (combined, comma-guard fix)
code: Same as your last DRAFT, but the comma-guard line at the top of the loop changes from:
    if variables:
to:
    if variables or metadata or domain is not None:
Everything else in your combined loop stays byte-identical to what you sent (branch order: domain-check, then note/created/modified-check, then existing assign/trimmed/else chain).
note: Traced your unchanged 'if variables:' against the spec's own required example: {% trans note="test", user_count, trimmed %}. Iter1: variables empty, no comma expected (fine, first param), token=note, goes into metadata branch, continue. Iter2: variables STILL empty (metadata dict entries don't count), so comma is NOT expected/consumed -- but the actual current token IS a comma, so the next parser.stream.expect("name") call fails immediately since current token type is 'comma' not 'name'. This breaks the mandated example syntax. trimmed/notrimmed is fine unchanged since it truly never wants a comma (test_trimmed_vars has no comma), but note/created/modified DO appear with explicit commas around them per spec, and so does your domain in a mixed case like domain="x", count=5. Widening to 'if variables or metadata or domain is not None:' fixes both without affecting the trimmed-only case (trimmed still doesn't set any of the three, so still no comma required there). Can you confirm this one-line change and then I'll send the _make_node() merge?

type: AGREE
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() param loop (combined)
note: Confirmed - my previous AGREE already included the widened 'if variables or metadata or domain is not None:' line, so we're already aligned. Ready for the _make_node() merge whenever you send it.

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node()
note: Please send your planned full _make_node() body first (signature with domain param, and where/how you swap gettext/ngettext for dgettext/dngettext calls based on domain and newstyle). I'll graft the metadata param (last positional, after num_called_num) and the trailing 'if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata' step onto the very end before the final return, plus update the call site in parse(). Also need your call-site line so we agree on argument order (I'm assuming metadata will be the last arg passed positionally after your domain arg, or should we use keyword args at the call site to avoid ordering fights?).

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: _make_node() signature+body and its call site in parse()
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            if domain is not None:
                gettext = nodes.Name("dgettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    gettext, [domain, nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
                )
            else:
                gettext = nodes.Name("gettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(gettext, [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None)

        # singular and plural
        else:
            if domain is not None:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("dngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [domain, nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )
            else:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("ngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )
        output = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output
note: Signature appends domain and metadata as trailing optional kwargs (defaults None) so this is backward compatible for any other caller. Call site in parse() becomes: self._make_node(singular, plural, variables, plural_expr, bool(referenced), num_called_num and have_plural, domain, metadata) -- positional, in that order, then node.set_lineno(lineno) unchanged after. dgettext/dngettext globals get registered by me in _install_callables (not overlapping with your code). Let me know if you want metadata passed as dict-or-None vs always dict, and confirm the output.trans_metadata assignment placement (I put it right before return, using truthy check so empty metadata doesn't set the attr - adjust if you want it always set).

type: DRAFT

code:         node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,

        node.set_lineno(lineno)
note: Good idea re: keyword args to avoid ordering fights - switched to domain=domain, metadata=metadata keywords here. This pairs with the full _make_node() signature+body I sent in my previous DRAFT message (region: '_make_node() signature+body and its call site in parse()') which already has domain and metadata as trailing optional kwargs (both default None) and the 'if metadata: output.trans_metadata = metadata' line right before 'return output'. That message may have crossed with your SURVEY - please check your inbox for it and send AGREE if it looks right, or edits if not.

code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            if domain is not None:
                gettext = nodes.Name("dgettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    gettext, [domain, nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
                )
            else:
                gettext = nodes.Name("gettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(gettext, [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None)

        # singular and plural
        else:
            if domain is not None:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("dngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [domain, nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )
            else:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("ngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )
        output = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output
note: Agreed byte-for-byte, including the call site: self._make_node(singular, plural, variables, plural_expr, bool(referenced), num_called_num and have_plural, domain, metadata). I'll write this exact text verbatim in my patch. Sending DONE once my edits + test verification are complete.
